title: Pythagorean Theorem
summary: For a right triangle in Euclidean geometry, the square of the hypotenuse equals the sum of the squares of the other two sides.
claim: For a right triangle on a flat Euclidean plane: a² + b² = c².
proof: A standard area proof constructs a square of side length a + b, places four identical right triangles inside it, and compares the remaining central square of side c with the outer square area. Expanding and subtracting the triangle areas gives a² + b² = c².
validation: Level 5 — Mathematical / Logical Proof. Valid within Euclidean geometry for right triangles.
reconstruction: Can be rediscovered with rope geometry, a flat surface, and a 3-4-5 right triangle.
